videos.title: Shape Patterns and Sequences | Geometry Patterns for Kids
videos.description: Discover patterns made with shapes! Learn how triangles, squares, circles, and other shapes form repeating and growing sequences.
learningObjectives: Identify repeating and growing patterns made with shapes
Continue shape sequences by finding the rule
keyConceptsCovered: Shapes can form repeating sequences (circle, square, circle, square)
Shapes can form growing sequences (1 triangle, 2 triangles, 3 triangles)
Shape patterns follow the same rules as number patterns

videos.title: Shape Sequences with Rotation and Flip | Moving Shape Patterns
videos.description: Watch how shapes can rotate, flip, and change position in a pattern. Learn to spot patterns where shapes turn, reflect, or slide to create sequences.
learningObjectives: Identify shape patterns involving rotation and reflection
Predict the next shape in a movement sequence
keyConceptsCovered: Rotation: shape turns clockwise or counterclockwise
Flip: shape reflects to create a mirror image
Slide: shape moves position without changing orientation

videos.title: Tessellations and Tiling Patterns | Shapes That Fit Together
videos.description: Explore how shapes fit together to cover a surface with no gaps! Learn about tessellations: repeating shape patterns used in floors, walls, and art.
keyConceptsCovered: Tessellation = shapes covering a surface with no gaps and no overlaps
Squares, triangles, and hexagons tessellate perfectly
Tessellations are shape patterns used in real life

videos.title: What Shape Comes Next? | Shape Sequence Quiz
videos.description: Test your shape pattern skills! Each question shows a shape sequence, and you need to figure out the rule and pick what comes next. Fun interactive quiz.
learningObjectives: Quickly identify the rule in shape sequences
keyConceptsCovered: Handling sequences with multiple attributes (shape, color, size)

videos.title: Design Your Own Shape Pattern | Creative Geometry Activity
videos.description: Create your own shape patterns and tessellations! Use triangles, squares, hexagons, and other shapes to design beautiful patterns on paper or a grid.
learningObjectives: Create original shape patterns using geometric shapes
keyConceptsCovered: Creating simple tessellations on grid paper
Combining shapes to make complex patterns

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track through shape sequences with tessellations and creative challenge

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of shape sequences with practice and creative activity

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core shape sequence concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: A shape pattern goes: Triangle pointing up, Triangle pointing right, Triangle pointing down, Triangle pointing left, Triangle pointing __. What direction is next?
questions.explanation: The triangle rotates clockwise: up, right, down, left. After left, the pattern repeats and goes back to up.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use pattern blocks or cut-out shapes to create sequences on desks. Have students describe the rule in words before extending. Videos 1-3 are essential for all students. Tessellation exploration works well as a group activity.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child notice shape patterns on floors, fabrics, and buildings. Use shape cut-outs or stamps to create patterns together. Ask 'What is the rule in this shape pattern?'
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Look at shapes carefully: are they changing size, color, position, or type? Find the rule first, then you can predict what comes next!
